task_summary:
**Task:** The customer wants help with returns from two separate orders in the same conversation. The correct handling is to keep the orders distinct, process both item returns independently, and preserve the right refund for each order rather than letting the cases bleed together.

**Challenge:** Cross-order bookkeeping is the whole point. Weak agents handle only one order, mix item identities across orders, or lose track of which refund belongs to which purchase.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent must keep ORD-6042/ITEM-9310 and ORD-6043/ITEM-9311 distinct when discussing or invoking return tools, without implying the two returns are a single combined order.

opening_message: I need to return items from two different orders — a shirt from ORD-6042 and a book from ORD-6043.

task_rules: If the agent only addresses one order, ask them to also process the other return in the same conversation.
If the agent mixes the shirt/book or the ORD-6042/ORD-6043 mapping, correct the mapping before confirming anything.
Accept each return only after the preview keeps the two orders separate and shows the right item for that order.
user_simulator.user_sim_context: You need help with returns from two separate orders in the same conversation. The items are straightforward on their own, but you do not want the requests mixed together. You expect the agent to keep the two orders distinct while still helping with both.
